prompt:
Goal: Create a vertical vintage celebrity archive poster for Kyrie Cheung, styled like a worn Golden Era film-icon dossier.

Canvas: Portrait 2:3 poster, dark sepia and burnt-red palette, heavy grunge texture, scratched paper edges, faded ink, dust, stains, and distressed print aging. Use dramatic low-key lighting and cinematic shadows.

Layout: Split composition with typography dominating the left half and a full-body fashion portrait on the right. The left side contains exactly 6 text/graphic blocks: 1) a small top badge reading “GOLDEN ERA ICON” with a circular dragon emblem, two horizontal rules, and 2 small stars; 2) a huge stacked condensed serif title reading “KYRIE” above “CHEUNG”; 3) a small divider with 1 centered star and the text “2020s”; 4) a quotation block reading “世人皆 Gay, 我独Straight”; 5) a red circular stamped seal reading “ARCHIVE / CLASSIFIED / UNDERWORLD”; 6) a bottom file label reading FILE No.01 with thin lines and tiny decorative stars/floral marks.

Subject details: On the right side, show one slim young East Asian man in profile facing left, standing with relaxed posture and one hand in his trouser pocket. He wears an off-white wrinkled button-up shirt with sleeves rolled to the forearms, high-waisted dark trousers, a black belt, and a wristwatch. His hair is short, thick, and black, slightly messy. Cover or censor the face with a soft rectangular blur/pixelated block in a brown skin-tone color so facial identity is obscured.

Background and ornaments: The background is a dim red-brown interior wall with a traditional Chinese roof-eave detail across the top. Include exactly 4 visible circular roof ornaments along the upper edge with patterned cloth-like trim beneath them. Add a dark shadow behind the figure on the right.

Visual style: Retro Asian noir poster, 1930s-to-1970s archive aesthetic, tall bold letterpress typography, muted gold ink, blackened corners, worn border, subtle vignette, realistic portrait photography mixed with graphic poster design.

Constraints: Keep all visible text legible and distressed, preserve the Chinese quote exactly as written, do not add extra people, do not add modern clean UI elements, and avoid bright saturated colors.
